message:
fix: macaroon preallocation DoS, health watcher atomicity, compress flush deferral

cx/macaroon: MacaroonToken::from_bytes now caps the Vec preallocation
for caveats to min(caveat_count, (remaining_bytes) / 3) instead of
trusting the wire-format caveat_count directly. A malicious token with
a large caveat_count field but minimal actual data could cause an
unbounded allocation attempt. Each caveat requires at least 3 bytes
on the wire (1 kind + 2 length), so the clamped capacity is always
sufficient for valid input.

grpc/health: watched_status_and_version for the server-level (empty
service name) watcher now holds the statuses read lock while reading
the global version counter, preventing an interleaving where
set_status bumps the version between the status read and the version
read, causing the watcher to pair a stale status with a new version
and miss the actual transition. The previous code called self.check()
which acquires and releases the lock independently of the version read.

HealthWatcher::status() now returns the last-seen status from the
watcher's own snapshot (self.last_status) instead of performing a
fresh read. This matches the gRPC Watch contract where the stream
position is defined by changed()/poll_status() calls, and a bare
status() between those calls should reflect the most recent observation.

http/compress: GzipCompressor and DeflateCompressor compress() methods
no longer call encoder.flush() after write_all. The flate2 write
encoder buffers internally and emits compressed bytes to the output
Vec as the buffer fills. Calling flush() after every chunk forces a
sync flush marker into the compressed stream, which inflates the output
for small chunks and prevents the encoder from optimizing across chunk
boundaries. finish() still flushes the final state. Test added:
gzip_compresses_repetitive_data_chunked verifies that 4000 bytes of
repetitive data compressed in 10-byte chunks achieves > 50% compression.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
